promotion_note: Promoted by the parked-class sweep (not by a shadow): the sweep generalized the parked Suggestions to their kinds and found an at-or-above-threshold sibling — the after_round read-boundary fail-open, the sibling of this PR's own _valid_override read guard.

fix_decisions.note: after_round read-boundary fail-open. record-revision now persists the floor it validated against; _validate re-checks against THAT (not a re-derivation, which has demonstrable false positives). Verified: exploit closed, correct refusal unchanged, floor-0-while-open positive control still legal.

stale_prose_check.rows: no STALE rows at post-fix HEAD

skipped_checks.reason: working tree dirty at the pre-commit run (grades committed HEAD). The independent stale-prose pre-check over origin/main...HEAD at the committed head reported zero STALE rows.

parked_class_sweep.seeds_excluded_reason: claim-quality pushbacks whose recorded evidence establishes the claim FALSE — a false class is not a useful enumeration seed; priming on it manufactures lookalikes rather than siblings.
sites_examined: scripts/issue-audit-state.py (whole file — it is wholly new, so the entire module is changed surface)

new_siblings.note: the ONLY fail-open sibling; promoted to this iteration and fixed

the second no-digest-supplied site is unreachable when overrides is non-empty; stale-override short-circuits it, and SKILL.md documents a remedy for no-digest-supplied but none for stale-override — the misattribution denies the run its self-correction. Fails closed.
query-summary renders a foreign nonce as state=unestablished with the cause only on stderr, while every sibling query puts reason=foreign-nonce on stdout
the pending-token domain is literal-duplicated at three sites with no shared accessor — the same shape _attestation_frozen was extracted to fix
the two-concurrently-open-rounds guard has zero test rows
the user-decline must-name-surface guard has zero test rows
record-degraded validates --reason against _DEGRADED_REASONS and echoes it but never PERSISTS it — the cause is permanently lost and _DEGRADED_REASONS is decorative

shadow.trigger: convergence-time (iteration 5 reached a tentative non-REJECT verdict after the sweep)
shadow.coverage_note: 1:1 join satisfied — all 6 dispatched identifiers returned exactly one parsed, evidence-bearing result. iterations:first-only NOT applied (never applies to a shadow), so code-reviewer dispatched. Provenance re-checked at shadow time: the prompt extension is neither locally modified nor in the reviewed diff, and no focus/scoping clause was added to any prompt — hence prompt_addenda: none.

phase3_findings.description: _validate iterates rnd.get('embed_markers', []) with no isinstance gate — the only round container without one (rounds/revisions/overrides/attempts/creation are all gated). The `, []` default covers an ABSENT key; a present-but-non-iterable value raises TypeError, not StateError. _query_state catches only StateError and main has no top-level guard, so it escapes as a raw traceback — breaking the two-class contract this diff asserts in three places (the module docstring's 'Query subcommands ALWAYS exit 0 ... A crashed read is never presented as a value'; the comment at :551-554 naming this 'the exact two-class-contract violation _validate exists to prevent'; and DEVFLOW_SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md:453). The state file is agent-mutable scratch, so CLAUDE.md's best-effort-parser wrong-type row is squarely in scope.
phase3_findings.reproduction: embed_markers: 5 -> `query-triggers` dies with TypeError: 'int' object is not iterable (raw traceback). Independently reproduced by the orchestrator.

phase3_findings.description: Same root cause, mutation side: rnd['embed_markers'] is indexed unconditionally at :1349-1350. _validate requires only ('round','attempts','outcome') on a round record and reads embed_markers via .get, so a hand-edited/truncated round lacking the key validates cleanly and record-dispatch raises KeyError — a raw traceback rather than the contract's named breadcrumb. A _validate-level list check that defaults the key closes both findings.

cmd_record_creation_attestation's trailing-newline retry swallows a _DigestError with a bare `except _DigestError: pass` and no breadcrumb. Benign for correctness (status already holds the conservative 'mismatch', so no false match is possible) — a diagnosability gap only.
_OVERRIDE_SURFACES declares three tokens but only t1t2-boundary is ever invoked; Step 4's decline path never calls record-override, so step4-offer / step4-approval-after-exhausted-offer are unwired.

The state document has no static type (TypedDict/dataclass); TRANSITIONS rows are bare dicts so a misspelled key reads None rather than erroring. Quality suggestions; no invariant violated on a reachable path.
record-degraded's 'no round N is recorded' guard is never driven (every sibling command's equivalent is); the --reason choices validation is untested.

_valid_override binds on a recorded digest whenever one exists rather than only on file-arm epochs, and derives file_arm_epoch from last_completed(state) rather than the override's own recorded round. Both fail closed; no live bug under current call patterns.

shadow.over_grade_gate: no promote-path finding flagged. The Critical is fail-OPEN in the contract sense (a raw traceback where the contract promises a decided token) — shape 1 explicitly excludes a fail-open defect; it is not diagnostic-only (shape 2) because the module's own diff-added prose asserts the contract it breaks; and it is from code-reviewer, not an empirical over-grader (shape 3). It therefore drives the verdict at its emitted severity with no calibration record required.
shadow.promotion_blocked_reason: ITERATION CAP: iteration 5 of max_iterations=5 has already run, so Decide outcome 2's cap branch fires — do NOT start a further iteration. The unresolved population contains a Critical, so the final verdict is REJECT.
